title: coreopsis bigelovii
intro: Coreopsis bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's tickseed, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Coreopsis bigelovii include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu. Coreopsis bigelovii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in folk medicine to promote overall well-being and support immune function.
